question: When searching process instances by multiple tags, how does the filtering behave?

options.text: OR semantics — instances matching any of the requested tags are returned.
AND semantics — instances must contain all requested tags and may contain additional ones; wildcard and partial matching are not supported.
Exact-set semantics — instances must contain exactly the requested tags and no others.
Prefix wildcard semantics — instances are returned when any tag starts with the requested string.

a.text: OR semantics is incorrect; tag search requires every requested tag to be present on the instance.

b.text: The docs state filtering uses AND semantics: an instance must contain all requested tags but may contain additional ones, and partial or wildcard matching is not supported.

c.text: An instance may contain additional tags beyond those in the query; the match is not constrained to an exact set.

d.text: The docs explicitly state partial or wildcard matching is not supported, so prefix matching is not allowed.

explanation: Process instance tag search filters with AND semantics: the instance must contain every requested tag, with extra tags allowed. Partial and wildcard matching are not supported, so callers must request exact tag values.
